hero.headline: Professional Resin Calculator for Woodworking Projects
hero.subtitle: From Photo to Precise Resin Estimate
home.welcomeStatement: Welcome to HFZWood — your workspace for resin estimation and woodworking knowledge.
home.welcomeSupporting: Use the navigation to start a new project, open saved work, or explore tutorials, glossary terms, and the knowledge base.
home.guestStatement: The first platform that gives woodworkers and resin enthusiasts both the tools to calculate exactly and the knowledge to work confidently.
home.guestSupporting: HFZWood combines photo-based resin estimation, project workflow, practical tutorials, a glossary and a knowledge base in one professional workspace.
home.guestAccountMessage: Create your free HFZWood account to unlock the complete platform.

preferences.intro: Personalize how HFZWood looks and how measurements are shown. These settings affect only this device and never change saved project data.

preferences.lengthUnitHelp: Used for measurement labels and calculator inputs. Internal calculations stay canonical.
preferences.volumeUnitHelp: Used for resin volume displays and calculator outputs.

calculator.referenceLengthPositive: Reference length must be greater than 0 ({unit}).
calculator.cavityDepthPositive: Cavity depth must be greater than 0 ({unit}).
calculator.mainPourDepthPositive: Main pour depth must be greater than 0 ({unit}).
calculator.title: River Table & Woodworking Resin Calculator
calculator.step1Body: For best results, upload a clear top-down photo of your project that includes the entire mold and all wood pieces.
calculator.step2Body: Draw reference measurements using known dimensions visible in the photo. These measurements are used to calibrate the image scale before calculating resin volume. For best accuracy, add multiple horizontal and vertical references, especially when the photo is not perfectly top-down.
calculator.referenceDraft: Reference draft captured. Enter its real-world length ({unit}):
calculator.step3Body: Draw the inside perimeter of the mold. This area will be used as the outer boundary for all resin calculations.
calculator.step4Body: Trace the outline of each wood slab inside the mold. These areas will be excluded from the resin volume calculation.
calculator.step5Body: Trace every resin area located between the mold boundary and the wood islands. Each cavity represents an area that will be included in the final resin volume calculation.
calculator.resultsOutdated: Results need recalculation after your latest edit.
calculator.help.photo.title: Photo Tips
calculator.help.photo.text:
For best results, photograph the project from directly above.
Include the complete mold and at least one known measurement.
calculator.help.reference.title: Reference Measurements
calculator.help.reference.text:
Add measurements in both horizontal and vertical directions whenever possible.
More reference measurements improve calculation accuracy.
calculator.help.mold.title: Mold Boundary
calculator.help.mold.text: Trace the inside edge of the mold that will contain the resin.
calculator.help.wood.title: Wood Islands
calculator.help.wood.text:
Trace each separate wood slab or island inside the mold.
Resin area is calculated as mold area minus the total wood island area.
calculator.help.cavity.title: Resin Cavities
calculator.help.cavity.text:
Trace cracks, voids, knots, or other cavities that will be filled with resin.
Each cavity can have its own depth.
calculator.help.mainResinDepth.title: Main Resin Depth
calculator.help.mainResinDepth.text: Enter the average depth of the main resin area between the wood slabs. This value is used to calculate the volume of the large resin-filled section of the project.
calculator.help.mainResinDepth.examples: Example: If the river section is approximately 50 mm deep, enter 50.
calculator.error.uploadImageBeforeSave: Upload an image before saving a project.
calculator.error.addReferenceBeforeConti: Add at least one reference measurement before continuing.
calculator.error.drawMoldBeforeContinue: Draw the mold boundary before continuing.
calculator.error.completeWoodIslandFirst: Complete the current wood island before finishing the Wood step.
calculator.error.addWoodIslandBeforeCont: Add at least one wood island before continuing.
calculator.error.woodIslandMinPoints: A wood island needs at least 3 points.
calculator.error.cavityMinPoints: A cavity needs at least 3 points.
calculator.error.calculateBeforePdf: Calculate results before exporting a PDF.
calculator.error.pdfExportUnavailable: PDF export is not available for new projects on this account.
calculator.error.polygonPointLimit: This {kind} is limited to {maxPoints} points for new projects on this account.
calculator.error.layerPlanningUnavailabl: Pour layer planning is not available for new projects on this account.
calculator.error.firstFillPlanningUnavai: First fill planning is not available for new projects on this account.
calculator.error.mainDepthBeforeLayers: Enter a valid Main Resin Depth before calculating layers.
calculator.error.maxPourThicknessPositiv: Maximum Pour Thickness must be greater than 0.
calculator.error.calculateVolumeBeforePl: Calculate Resin Volume first to set the resin surface area.
calculator.error.firstFillThicknessRange: First Fill Seal Coat Thickness must be greater than 0 and not exceed Main Resin Depth.
calculator.error.firstFillThicknessPosit: First Fill Seal Coat Thickness must be greater than 0.
calculator.planning.optionalToolsSubtitl: Use these planning aids when helpful. They do not change the resin volume calculation.
calculator.planning.firstFillSealedUnder: Wood sealed underneath (silicone seal underneath wood)
calculator.planning.firstFillUnsealedUnd: Wood not sealed underneath (resin may leak underneath wood)
calculator.planning.firstFillTableHelper: This selection controls the First Fill Seal Coat row in the Pour Layer Planning table.
calculator.planning.layerBalanceNote: Layer thicknesses are automatically balanced to avoid very thin final pours.
calculator.help.firstFill.title: First Fill Seal Coat Calculator
calculator.help.firstFill.text: Enter the thickness of the initial sealing layer. The application will calculate the amount of resin needed for the first fill coat used to seal pores, cracks and potential leak paths before the main pour.
calculator.help.pourLayer.title: Pour Layer Planning
calculator.help.pourLayer.text1: Enter the maximum pour thickness recommended by the resin manufacturer for the resin system you are using.
calculator.help.pourLayer.text2: The application will use any entered first fill seal coat and split the remaining depth into safe pour layers.
